title: A Fruitful Past Decade and a Hope-filled New Decade

audience_match: The CJ and other Supreme Court and appellate-court justices (sitting and retired)
Members of the three FLP Boards of Judges
FLP's institutional partners (TYKF, MPIC, Ayala, Metrobank)
FLP scholars, fellows, dissertation-contest winners, and FLPSS members
Deans and professors of Philippine law and business schools

keywords: MIBL Master in International Business Law
MIBL Miguel Panganiban Sandejas Babson Northwestern
primary_topics: Closing remarks at FLP's online Awards Ceremony, August 11, 2022, shifted from face-to-face at AIM due to Omicron
Inventory of FLP's three Board-of-Judges chairs: CJ Gesmundo (Scholarship), J. Hernando (Dissertation), Gov. Tetangco (ESMEL)
Origin story of the Master in International Business Law (MIBL) program proposed to AIM — derived from grandson Miguel Panganiban Sandejas's Babson MBA + Northwestern MSL trajectory
Decade-transition framing: FLP's first decade (2011-2021) concentrated on LIBERTY programs; second decade (2021-2031) will add PROSPERITY programs starting with ESMEL fellowships

Announcement of the two ultimate projects: Center for Liberty and Prosperity (with interactive immersive Museum) in partnership with the SC; and the 'Entrepreneurship Fund' of at least ₱1 billion for MSMEs
CJ Gesmundo quoted as articulating the FLP symbiosis: 'safeguarding rights go hand in hand with nurturing prosperity'
sub_topics: Diplomatic protocol opener — Chief Justice + other justices, three appellate courts, diplomatic corps, CBCP, three FLP Boards of Judges, FLPSS

Quoted summary from CJ Gesmundo's keynote: 'By safeguarding the rights and freedoms of the individual, the judiciary allows our country to prosper, and in nurturing economic prosperity, rights will also be securely protected. Indeed, it is a symbiotic relationship.'
Justice Ramon Paul L. Hernando chairing the Dissertation Writing Contest Board of Judges
Gov. Amando M. Tetangco Jr. chairing the new ESMEL Fellowship Board of Judges, FLP trustee
ESMEL fellowships at ₱450,000 each — first explicit prosperity-side program
MIBL (Master in International Business Law) program designed with AIM President/Dean Jikyeong Kang and AIM faculty Professors Olivier Roche and Felipe Calderon over 8 months

Grandson Miguel Panganiban Sandejas — MBA magna cum laude from Babson College Boston (entrepreneurship), Master of Science in Law (MSL) with honors from Northwestern University Chicago — no undergraduate law degree, cannot take PH bar
Aspirational curriculum: business + law + technology fusion; obligations and contracts, patents, trademarks, data privacy, property rights, statutory construction
Hope that Ateneo, La Salle, UP, UST will follow AIM's lead
Metro Pacific Investments Corporation (MPIC) and MPI Foundation led by MVP as ESMEL partner; CFO Chaye Cabal-Revilla and bar-topper Chief Legal Counsel Ricardo 'Jun' Pilares III drafted the MOA
Independent-director disclosure: CJP discloses he is an Independent Director of MPIC (corporate governance disclosure)
Tan Yan Kee Foundation led by Dr. Lucio C. Tan — six years of Scholarship Program funding even through COVID; funded 21 (not 20) scholars to resolve a tie for 10th place
Ayala Corporation led by Jaime Augusto Zobel de Ayala — five-year Dissertation Writing Contest partner; will expand to ESMEL students next year
Tony Tan Caktiong and Injap Sia — jointly donated a Hotel 101 Taguig condo to FLP, income for the Panganiban Education Assistance Program
Professorial Chairs Program with Metrobank Foundation — suspended during COVID, awaiting safe restart for face-to-face lectures
Justice Angelina Sandoval Gutierrez — Acting FLP President, President of Association of Retired Supreme Court Justices
Cezar Consing (former BPI/BAP president) — pro bono study of viability and sustainability of the Entrepreneurship Fund

stances.claim: FLP's first decade (2011-2021) was correctly focused on liberty programs (Professorial Chairs, Law Scholarships, Dissertation Contests); the second decade (2021-2031) must broaden to prosperity programs (ESMEL Fellowships, Entrepreneurship Fund) to enact the full twin-beacons philosophy.
stances.rhetorical_move: decade-transition framing as the structural argument of the speech

stances.claim: The best way to nurture prosperity is to help people help themselves through private entrepreneurship — not state-led intervention, not charity transfers — therefore FLP's ultimate prosperity-side project is an 'Entrepreneurship Fund' of at least ₱1 billion that INVESTS in and helps manage MSMEs, with active assistance from ESMEL fellows.

Filipino business schools should fuse business, law, and technology in their curricula — modeled after CJP's grandson Miguel's Babson MBA + Northwestern MSL trajectory — so that entrepreneurs are armed with both business expertise AND legal grounding in obligations, contracts, patents, trademarks, data privacy, property rights, and statutory construction.
stances.confidence: asserted with named AIM faculty partners

stances.claim: CJ Gesmundo's keynote-articulated principle — 'safeguarding the rights and freedoms of the individual, the judiciary allows our country to prosper, and in nurturing economic prosperity, rights will also be securely protected... safeguarding rights go hand in hand with nurturing prosperity' — should be received as the canonical SC-articulation of FLP's symbiotic-philosophy and as evidence that the Chief Justice now subscribes to it.
stances.rhetorical_move: endorsement-by-quotation — making the sitting CJ a co-author of the philosophy
stances.confidence: asserted with explicit 'and by we I respectfully include him'

notable_anecdotes.summary: The MIBL origin: CJP's grandson Miguel Panganiban Sandejas finished MBA magna cum laude in entrepreneurship at Babson College Boston, then completed Master of Science in Law (MSL) with honors at Northwestern University Chicago — WITHOUT any prior law degree. He cannot take the PH bar (which requires a four-year law degree). This unusual graduate-level law-without-undergraduate trajectory inspired the AIM MIBL curriculum.
Tan Yan Kee Foundation's gesture: facing a tie for 10th place among scholarship applicants, TYKF added one more third-year slot, raising the annual scholarship count from 20 to 21.
Tony Tan Caktiong and Injap Sia jointly donated a Hotel 101 Taguig City condo to FLP — income to fund the Panganiban Education Assistance Program for poor students.
Cezar Consing, former BPI president and former Bankers Association of the Philippines president, is doing PRO BONO viability-and-sustainability study of the planned ₱1 billion Entrepreneurship Fund.

Quoted institutional crystallization from CJ Gesmundo: 'By safeguarding the rights and freedoms of the individual, the judiciary allows our country to prosper, and in nurturing economic prosperity, rights will also be securely protected. Indeed, it is a symbiotic relationship, safeguarding rights go hand in hand with nurturing prosperity.' CJP comments: 'So well said, Your Honor.'

Corporate-governance disclosure interpolated mid-speech: 'As a measure of good corporate governance, may I disclose that I am an Independent Director of MPIC?' — modeling the disclosure norm he wants to inculcate.

decision_framework_signals: uses decade-transition framing to structure long-horizon institutional planning
treats helping-people-help-themselves-via-entrepreneurship as the operative anti-poverty test
fuses business + law + technology as the new curriculum-design test for graduate management education
uses sitting-CJ endorsement-by-quotation to legitimize foundation philosophy
models corporate-governance disclosure ('may I disclose') as itself an FLP value-statement
scales prosperity-side ambition by ₱-figures (₱450K per fellowship, ₱1B+ for the Entrepreneurship Fund)

one_paragraph_summary: Delivered August 11, 2022 as online closing remarks at FLP's Awards Ceremony — shifted from face-to-face at AIM to virtual format because of the Omicron surge — this speech frames the foundation's decade-transition thesis: the first decade (2011-2021) was concentrated on LIBERTY programs (Professorial Chairs with Metrobank, Scholarship with Tan Yan Kee Foundation, Dissertation Writing with Ayala); the second decade (2021-2031) will broaden into PROSPERITY programs starting with the ESMEL Fellowships at ₱450,000 each (in partnership with MPIC and MVP, MOA drafted by CFO Chaye Cabal-Revilla and bar-topper Chief Legal Counsel Jun Pilares III). CJP discloses his MPIC independent directorship as a corporate-governance modeling. He thanks CJ Alexander G. Gesmundo as Scholarship Board of Judges chair and adopts the CJ's keynote formulation — 'safeguarding rights go hand in hand with nurturing prosperity' — as the canonical SC-articulation of FLP's symbiotic philosophy. He thanks J. Ramon Paul L. Hernando (Dissertation Board) and Gov. Amando 'Say' Tetangco Jr. (ESMEL Board). He then announces the AIM Master in International Business Law (MIBL) curriculum, designed over 8 months with President/Dean Jikyeong Kang and faculty Olivier Roche and Felipe Calderon — inspired by his grandson Miguel Panganiban Sandejas's unusual Babson MBA + Northwestern MSL trajectory (graduate law without undergraduate law) — and invites Ateneo, La Salle, UP, and UST to follow AIM's lead. He acknowledges Tan Yan Kee Foundation (six years of scholarship funding, including 21-slot expansion to resolve a tie), Ayala Corporation (5 years of Dissertation Writing), Tony Tan Caktiong + Injap Sia (Hotel 101 condo donation for poor-student assistance), and Justice Angelina Sandoval Gutierrez (Acting FLP President). He closes by announcing FLP's two ultimate projects — the Center for Liberty and Prosperity with interactive immersive Museum (liberty side, with SC partnership) and the ₱1B+ 'Entrepreneurship Fund' for MSMEs (prosperity side, with pro bono viability study by Cezar Consing) — and asks the audience to pray for their attainment.
